lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Three minutes to six and the break room smells of burnt coffee and the particular exhaustion that has no name. Theo sits at the small table. The form is in front of him.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He uncaps the pen. He finds the date field. February. The number. The year that has been going on a long time now.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He writes it. Carefully. The way you write something when you want the act to count for something even if nothing follows from it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He looks at the signature line.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The line is empty. It has always been empty. It will remain so for another six seconds, another thirty, another minute he does not count precisely. At some point the six seconds becomes a fact he is simply living inside of.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: From the corridor, someone calls a bay number. A cart rolls past. The shift does not pause to ask what he is doing at the table. The shift has never asked. That is, he understands now, one of its qualities.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He folds the form once. Then again. The crease goes over the date, not over the signature line. He notices this. He cannot say why it matters. It does.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He says it to no one in the room. He is confirming something. A weight he now carries with a shape, a specific mass, a room number on the fourth floor he looked up through the back channel forty minutes ago.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He puts the folded form in his breast pocket. Over the sternum. Where the ribs converge.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Outside the window the sky is the gray of something that has not decided. February does that. Holds the dark a little longer than it has to, then releases it without ceremony.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He stands. He straightens his badge. He takes a breath that does not resolve into anything dramatic, that does not mark a turning point, that is simply a breath a man takes before going back out.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The form is dated. The form is unsigned. It is in his pocket rather than his locker, which is a distance of thirty-four feet and several months, and he is aware of this, and he goes back to work.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The corridor takes him. The board has a new entry. A patient in Bay Four needs a line. Bay Eight is asking about discharge paperwork. The floor runs exactly as it has run all night: completely, without asking, without rest.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Three minutes to six becomes six. Six becomes six-fifteen. The name on the fourth floor has not changed. Neither has the one on the form.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The shift continues around him. He lets it.
